response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a full-stack web and TypeScript/Node.js developer with roughly 15 years of experience, but her profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. Her C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a student-era audio synthesis project — nowhere near the 5–10 years of hands-on C++ required, and there is no evidence of Windows-platform C++ work. She has no SQL Server or Oracle experience (her database background is MongoDB and generic SQL), and she has no meaningful exposure to fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking systems. RIDE Capital touches investment practices at a high level but is not a capital-markets engineering context.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background with demonstrated ability to learn new stacks quickly across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency systems
Strong CI/CD and DevOps experience (GitLab, Docker, Artifactory, trunk-based development) which maps to the optional desirable skills
Java background present and some experience with high-throughput, low-latency service design at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months professionally, 2014–2015) versus the mandatory 5–10 years required, with no evidence of sustained Windows-platform C++ work
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary database exposure is MongoDB, which is irrelevant to the role's mandatory SQL requirements
Zero domain exposure to fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking systems
